title: Fine granularity refresh
abstract: A data processing system includes a memory channel and a data processor coupled to the memory channel. The data processor is adapted to access at least one rank and has refresh logic. In response to an activation of the refresh logic, the data processor generates refresh cycles to a bank. The data processor selects one of a first state corresponding to a first auto-refresh command that causes the data processor to auto-refresh the bank, and a second state corresponding to a second auto-refresh command that causes the data processor to auto-refresh a selected subset of the bank. The data processor initiates a switch between the first state and the second state in response to the refresh logic detecting a first condition related to the bank, and between the second state and the first state in response to the refresh logic circuit detecting a second condition.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A data processing system (100), comprising: a memory channel (130) comprising at least one rank (134/ 136/ 138); and a data processor (110) coupled to said memory channel (130) and adapted to access said at least one rank (134/ 136/ 138), said data processor (110) having refresh logic (532), wherein in response to an activation of said refresh logic (532), said data processor (110): generates refresh cycles to a bank (702) of said memory channel (130), wherein said data processor (110) selects one of a first state (604) and a second state (606/608), wherein said first state (604) corresponds to a first auto-refresh command (700) that causes said data processor (110) to auto-refresh said bank (702), and said second state corresponds to a second auto-refresh command (710/720) that causes said data processor (110) to auto-refresh a selected subset of said bank (702); initiates a switch between said first state (604) and said second state (606/608) in response to said refresh logic (532) detecting a first condition (618/622) related to said bank (702); and initiates a switch between said second state (606/608) and said first state (604) in response to a detection of a second condition (620/624). The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said refresh logic (532) detects said first condition (816) in response to a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702) being above a predetermined threshold. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein: said refresh logic (532) detects said second condition (810) in response to a number of refreshes sent to said bank (702) corresponds to a total number of subsets of said bank (702); and said data processor stores no access request to said bank (702). The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein: said data processor (110) refreshes a first subset (714) and a second subset (716) of said bank (702) within a refresh cycle time when said second auto-refresh command is a refresh two (REF2) command; and each of said first subset (714) and said second subset (716) of said bank (702) is a separate half subset of said bank (702). The data processing system (100) of claim 4, wherein: said data processor (110) refreshes said first subset (724), said second subset (726), a third subset (728), 

 and a fourth subset (730) of said bank (702) within said refresh cycle time when said second auto- refresh command is a refresh four (REF4) command; and each of said first subset (724), said second subset (726), said third subset (728), and said fourth subset (740) of said bank (702) is a separate quarter subset of said bank (702). 6. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said data processor (110) detects said first condition (618/622) in response to one or more of: a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702), a number of pending requests to said bank (702), a priority of said pending requests to said bank (702), and a type of said pending requests to said bank (702). 7. The data processing system (100) of claim 6, wherein said data processor (110) tracks said number of pending refreshes to said bank (702) to determine an initiation of a switch between said first state (604) and said second state (606/608). 8. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of said bank (702) of one rank (134). 9. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of a plurality of banks of one rank (134). 10. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of a corresponding bank of each of a plurality of ranks (134/136/138). 11. The data processing system (100) of claim 1, wherein said memory channel (130) comprises a plurality of ranks (134/136/138) of double rate (DDR) version four (DDR4) memory. 12. A data processor (110/200) comprising: a memory accessing agent (210/220); and a memory controller (292/500) coupled to said memory accessing agent (210/22) and adapted to couple to a memory system (120), wherein said memory controller (292/500): comprises a refresh logic circuit (532) for generating refresh cycles to a memory of the memory system (120), having an on-the-fly mode wherein said on-the-fly mode comprises a first state (604) and a second state (606/608), said first state (604) corresponding to a first auto-refresh command (700) that causes said memory to auto-refresh a bank (702), said second state (606/608) corresponding to a second auto-refresh command (710/720) that causes said memory to auto-refresh a selected subset of said bank (702); 

 switches between said first state (604) and said second state (606/608) in response to said refresh logic circuit (532) detecting a first condition (618/622) related to said bank (702) of said memory system (120); and switches between said second state (606/608) and said first state (604) in response to refresh logic circuit (532) detecting a second condition (620/624). 13. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) detects said first condition (620/624) in response to a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702) being above a predetermined threshold. 14. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) detects said second condition (620/624) in response to a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702). 15. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein: said memory controller (292/500) is adapted to refresh a first subset (714) and a second subset (716) of said bank (702) within a refresh cycle time, when said second auto-refresh command (710/720) is a refresh two (REF2) command (710); and each of said first subset (714) and said second subset (716) of said bank (702) is a separate half subset of said bank (702). 16. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein: said memory controller (292/500) is adapted to refresh a first subset (724), a second subset (726), a third subset (728), and a fourth subset (730) of said bank (702) within a refresh cycle time when said second auto-refresh command (710/720) is a refresh four (REF4) command (720); and each of said first subset (724), said second subset (726), said third subset (728), and said fourth subset (740) of said bank (702) is a separate quarter subset of said bank (720). 17. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said memory controller (292/500) detects said first condition (618/622) in response to one or more of: a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702), a number of pending requests to said bank (702), a priority of said pending requests to said bank (702), and a type of said pending requests to said bank (702). 18. The data processor (110/200) of claim 17, wherein said memory controller (292/500) tracks said number of pending refreshes to said bank (702) to determine an initiation of a switch between said first state (604) and said second state (606/608). 19. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of said bank (702) of one rank (134). 20. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of a plurality of banks of one rank (134). 21. The data processor (110/200) of claim 12, wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/720) refreshes said selected subset of a corresponding bank of each of a plurality of ranks (134/136/138). 22. A method (800) for managing refresh of a memory in a memory system (120) via a memory controller (292/500), the method comprising: generating a first auto-refresh command (700) when the memory controller (292/500) is in a first state (604/802), wherein said first auto-refresh command (700) causes the memory to auto-refresh a bank (702), generating a second auto-refresh command (710/712) when the memory controller (292/500) is in a second state (606/608/802), wherein said second auto-refresh command (710/712) causes the memory to auto-refresh a selected subset of said bank (702), switching between said first state and said second state in response to the memory controller (292/500) detecting a first condition (612/618/806/808) related to said bank (702); and switching between said second state and said first state in response to the memory controller (292/500) detecting a second condition (620/624/810/812). 23. The method (800) of claim 22, wherein said detecting said first condition comprises: detecting when a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702) are above a predetermined threshold. 24. The method (800) of claim 22, wherein said detecting said second condition comprises: detecting that a number of refreshes sent corresponds to a total number of subsets of said bank (702); and detecting that the memory controller (292/500) stores no access request to said bank (702). 25. The method (800) of claim 22, wherein generating said second auto-refresh command comprises: refreshing a first subset (714) and a second subset (716) of said bank (702) within a refresh period when said second auto-refresh command is a refresh two (REF2) command, (710), wherein each of said first subset (714) and said second subset (716) of said bank (702) is a separate half subset of said bank (702). 26. The method (800) of claim 22, wherein generating said second auto-refresh command comprises: refreshing a first subset (724), a second subset (726), a third subset (728), and a fourth subset (730) of said 

 bank (702) within a refresh period when said second auto-refresh command is a refresh two (REF2) command, wherein said first subset (724), said second subset (726), said third subset (728), and said fourth subset (724) of said bank is a separate quarter subset of said bank (702). 27. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising: detecting said first condition in response to one or more of: a number of pending refreshes to said bank (702), a number of pending requests to said bank (702), a priority of said pending requests to said bank (702), and a type of said pending requests to said bank (702). 28. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising refreshing said selected subset of said bank (702) of one rank (134) in response to said generating said second auto-refresh command. 29. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising refreshing said selected subset of a plurality of banks of one rank (134) in response to said generating said second auto-refresh command. 30. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising refreshing said selected subset of a corresponding bank of each of a plurality of ranks (134/136/138) in response to said generating said second auto-refresh command. 31. The method (800) of claim 22, further comprising: performing said generating said first auto-refresh command (700), said generating said second auto-refresh command (710/712), said switching between said first state and said second state, and said switching between said second state and said first state independently for each of a plurality of banks of the memory. 32. A memory controller (500) having an input for receiving memory access requests, and an output for providing memory accesses to a memory system (120), the memory controller (500) comprising: a command queue (520) for receiving the memory access requests and having a plurality of entries and an output for providing selected memory accesses; an arbiter (538) coupled to said command queue (520) for selecting entries from said command queue (538) for dispatch to the memory system (120); and a refresh logic circuit (532) coupled to said arbiter (514), wherein in an on-the-fly refresh mode, said refresh logic circuit (532) generates auto-refresh commands to the memory system (120) with a granularity that it automatically selects in response to conditions associated with a memory bank to be refreshed and issues a number of auto-refresh commands during a refresh interval that is inversely proportional to said granularity. 33. The memory controller (500) of claim 32, wherein when in a first state (604), said refresh logic circuit (532) provides a first auto-refresh command (REF1) during said refresh interval that causes a memory to auto-refresh all of said memory bank, and when in a second state (606), said refresh logic circuit (532) provides multiple auto-refresh commands during said refresh interval to auto-refresh a portion of said memory bank corresponding to said multiple auto-refresh commands. 34. The memory controller (500) of claim 33, wherein when in a second state (606), said refresh logic circuit (532) provides two second auto-refresh commands (REF2) during said refresh interval each of which causes said memory to auto-refresh one-half of said memory bank. 35. The memory controller (500) of claim 34, wherein when in a third state (608), said refresh logic circuit (532) provides four third auto-refresh commands (REF4) during said refresh interval each of which causes said memory to auto-refresh one-fourth of said memory bank. 36. The memory controller (500) of claim 33, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) transitions between said first state (604) and said second state (606) in response to one or more of: a number of pending refreshes to said bank, a number of pending requests to said bank, a priority of pending requests to said memory bank, and a type of pending requests to said bank. 37. The memory controller (500) of claim 33, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) transitions between said first state (604) and said second state (606) in response to a number of pending memory access requests to said memory bank being above a predetermined threshold. 38. The memory controller (500) of claim 33, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) transitions between said second state (606) and said first state (604) only after issuing a number of auto-refresh commands corresponding to a portion of said memory bank that is being refreshed at one time. 39. The memory controller (500) of claim 38, wherein said refresh logic circuit (532) transitions between said second state (606) and said first state (604) further when said command queue (520) stores no memory access request to said memory bank. 40. The memory controller (500) of claim 32, wherein in said on-the-fly refresh mode, said refresh logic circuit (532) generates auto-refresh commands to the memory system (120) with said granularity that it automatically selects for all banks in a given rank of the memory system (120). 41. The memory controller (500) of claim 32, wherein in said on-the-fly refresh mode, said refresh logic circuit (532) generates auto-refresh commands to the memory system (120) with said granularity that it automatically 

 selects for corresponding banks in multiple ranks of the memory system (120). 42. A memory controller (500) comprising: an interface (512) having an input for receiving memory access requests, and an output; a decoder (522) coupled to said output of said interface (512) for decoding said memory access requests and providing corresponding memory accesses in response; a command queue (520) having a first input coupled to said output of said decoder (522), a second input, and an output, and having a plurality of entries; an arbiter (538) coupled to said command queue (520) for providing auto-refresh commands to said second input of said command queue (520) selecting entries from said command queue (538) for dispatch to a memory system (120); a second queue (514) having an input coupled to said output of said command queue (520), and an output for coupling to a memory channel; and a refresh logic circuit (532) coupled to said arbiter (514), wherein in an on-the-fly refresh mode, said refresh logic circuit (532) generates auto-refresh commands to the memory system (120) with a granularity that it automatically selects in response to conditions associated with a memory bank to be refreshed and issues a number of auto-refresh commands during a refresh interval that is inversely proportional to said granularity, and provides said auto-refresh commands to said arbiter (538). 43. A method controlling accesses to a memory, comprising: receiving memory access requests; decoding the memory access requests to provide decoded memory access requests; storing the decoded memory access requests in a command queue (520); storing auto-refresh commands in said command queue (520); arbitrating among said decoded memory access requests and said auto-refresh commands in said command queue (520) to provide selected ones of said decoded memory access requests and said auto- refresh commands to the memory; and generating said auto-refresh commands in an on-the-fly refresh mode with a granularity that is automatically selected in response to conditions associated with a memory bank to be refreshed.
